summary: Comprehensive RBAC testing completed for MVPLine backend. All 31 role-based access control tests passed (100%). Tested data filtering for contacts, tasks, campaigns, calls, and conversations across all 4 user roles: super_admin, org_admin, store_manager, and user.
critical_code_review_comments: RBAC implementation is well-structured with clear separation of concerns (get_user_by_id, get_accessible_user_ids, get_data_filter)
Note: Super admin fetches ALL users on every request (line 62) - may need optimization with caching for large deployments
The get_data_filter function correctly handles edge cases: non-existent users return __NONE__ filter, invalid ObjectIds are caught
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_rbac.py - Created comprehensive RBAC test suite (31 tests)

org_admin.can_see: Data from users in their organization only

store_manager.can_see: Data from users in their store only

seed_data_creation: Created store_manager (69963f059eb007396fc5c496) and store_user (69963f059eb007396fc5c497) for complete role hierarchy testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: RBAC is fully implemented and tested. All 4 roles (super_admin, org_admin, store_manager, user) correctly filter data across contacts, tasks, campaigns, calls, and conversations. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_rbac.py covers all endpoints.

verified_features: Super admin sees ALL data across all organizations
Org admin sees data from their organization only
Store manager sees data from their store only
Regular user sees only their own data
External user (no org) sees only their own data
Non-existent user ID returns empty results

mocked_api.noted: Twilio SMS and OpenAI AI suggestions are MOCKED - this does not affect RBAC testing
